hotspots.title: Magyar’s EU funds deal and Sovereignty Office abolition
hotspots.blurb: Hungary’s incoming PM Péter Magyar secured a May 25 deadline to finalize a political deal with the EU to unlock €18 billion in frozen funds. His government also plans to abolish the controversial Sovereignty Protection Office, a key agency under Orbán’s rule, saving an estimated 6 billion forints annually.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the high stakes of EU funding for Hungary’s economy and the symbolic dismantling of Orbán-era institutions, which carries moral and political weight.

hotspots.title: Jordan Bardella’s fuel tax and EU flag standoff
hotspots.blurb: France’s far-right RN leader Jordan Bardella pledged to replace the EU flag with the French tricolor at Élysée and Matignon if elected president in 2027. He also supports taxing TotalEnergies’ record Q1 profits but faces internal party divisions over the policy.
hotspots.affect: This story dominates coverage due to Bardella’s rising prominence as a potential future president and the symbolic clash over national sovereignty versus EU integration, which resonates deeply in French politics.

hotspots.title: Trump administration’s assault on ABC and Jimmy Kimmel
hotspots.blurb: The Trump administration escalated its feud with ABC and late-night host Jimmy Kimmel, demanding his firing after a joke about Melania Trump. The FCC ordered an early review of Disney’s broadcast licenses, raising concerns about political interference in media.
hotspots.affect: This story is highly charged due to the perceived attack on free speech and media independence, which resonates with broader concerns about authoritarianism and political retaliation.

hotspots.title: PP-Vox ‘national priority’ pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Spain’s PP and far-right Vox formed coalition governments in Aragón and Extremadura, implementing Vox’s ‘national priority’ policy, which prioritizes Spaniards in public aid and housing. The pacts have sparked backlash from opposition parties and unions.
hotspots.affect: This story is intensely debated due to its implications for social equity, immigration policy, and the normalization of far-right policies in mainstream governance, which carries significant moral and ideological weight.

hotspots.title: PiS internal feud over Łukasz Mejza’s expulsion
hotspots.blurb: Poland’s ruling PiS party expelled controversial MP Łukasz Mejza from its parliamentary club after he accumulated 168 traffic penalty points and considered participating in freak fight events. The move has exposed internal divisions within the party.
hotspots.affect: This story commands attention due to the spectacle of a ruling party expelling a high-profile but controversial member, which highlights internal instability and moral failings within PiS.
